workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: I have this exec meeting notes from Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery that needs identify the blockers. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 16:16 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 09:18 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery pipeline hygiene

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 10:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery pipeline hygiene

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-N trap (Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-N failure mode (Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-N -- Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
